item.title: Trump's obsession with SAVE America Act drives Congress into standoff
item.description: The president and his conservative allies have stymied other legislation as they unsuccessfully try to pass a voting regulations bill that lacks even simple majority support in the Senate.

item.title: Fed's preferred inflation index hit 4.1% in May amid the war with Iran
item.description: May's personal consumption expenditure index, the Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge, rose 4.1% year to year, the highest level in three years. CBS News senior business and tech correspondent Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: Oil prices fall close to pre-war levels, but gas still up by about $1 nationally
item.description: As more ships resume passage through the Strait of Hormuz, oil prices are falling to near pre-war levels, but Americans are not seeing the same trend at the gas pump. CBS News contributor Javier David has more.

item.title: Sen. Bill Cassidy explains why he changed position on Iran war powers vote
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday rejected a measure to constrain President Trump's war powers in Iran after two key Republicans flipped their votes. CBS News chief foreign affairs correspondent and "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan spoke with one of those senators, Louisiana's Bill Cassidy, about the decision. Political strategists Matt Bennett and Doug Heye join with analysis.

item.title: Breaking down Supreme Court's immigration rulings favorable to Trump
item.description: The Supreme Court's conservative majority on Thursday backed restrictive Trump administration policies affecting temporary protected status for some migrants. The high court also sided with the administration on the methods of seeking asylum. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks down the cases.

item.title: Democrats rally for Talarico at Texas convention
item.description: Democrats from around the country are convening in Texas for the party's largest gathering this year. A top focus of the three-day convention will be on the state's upcoming Senate race. CBS News political contributor and Talarico campaign adviser Chuck Rocha joins to assess the state of the race.

item.title: Rep. Brad Schneider says Democrats "need to address the messaging" of Democratic Socialists
item.description: The establishment wing of the Democratic Party took a hit following Tuesday's primaries, in which Zohran Mamdani-backed challengers defeated Reps. Dan Goldman and Adriano Espaillat. Democratic Rep. Brad Schneider of Illinois, chair of the New Democrat Coalition, joins to assess the state of his party.

item.title: Jury remains deadlocked in trial of Palisades Fire arson suspect
item.description: A jury remains deadlocked in the trial of Jonathan Rinderknecht, the man accused of starting the blaze that turned into the deadly and destructive Palisades Fire in 2025.

item.title: 6-alarm fire destroys old industrial building in Allentown, Pennsylvania
item.description: A massive fire in Allentown, Pennsylvania, forced nearby residents to evacuate their homes Wednesday night.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Iran strikes ship in Strait of Hormuz in challenge to U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: Iran attacked a commercial vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday, a U.S. official confirmed, leading a United Nations agency to pause an evacuation effort.

item.title: Unclear if or when Trump will sign housing bill as SAVE Act fight continues
item.description: President Trump says he won't sign a bipartisan housing bill until the Senate passes the SAVE America Act. NOTUS congressional reporter Paul Kane joins with the latest from Capitol Hill.

item.title: Iran strikes vessel in Strait of Hormuz amid fee debate
item.description: Iran hit a cargo ship with a projectile while transiting the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday. Former CIA officer and Marine Corps veteran Elliot Ackerman joins with analysis.

item.title: Venezuelan earthquakes among strongest to hit country in over a century
item.description: Rescue efforts in Venezuela are underway after two deadly earthquakes hit on Wednesday. At least 188 people are dead, 1,500 are injured and 157 are missing, according to Venezuelan officials. The BBC's Vanessa Silva reports and Dr. Lucy Jones, a seismologist, has more.

item.title: How much water does AI really use?
item.description: The heaviest demand on America's water supply isn't data centers or AI. It's from everyday uses such as growing food, watering lawns and flushing toilets.

item.title: Trump calls on House Republicans to unify after meeting with Speaker Mike Johnson
item.description: President Trump continues to demand legislation requiring proof of citizenship for voter registration and voter identification at polling sites. That's driven Congress into a standoff. On social media Thursday, the president told House Republicans to unify. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe reports.

item.title: Fed's preferred inflation gauge hits highest level since 2023
item.description: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation gauge climbed to 4.1% in May, its highest level in more than three years. Josh Schafer, Barron's Investor Circle Newsletter editor, joins with analysis.

item.title: IFRC official on efforts to help Venezuela earthquake victims
item.description: Rescue efforts in Venezuela are underway after two powerful earthquakes struck the country on Wednesday night. Loyce Pace, International Federation of Red Cross and Red Crescent Societies regional director for the Americas, joins CBS News to discuss the state of aid.

item.title: Taylor Swift wedding venue speculation fueled by MSG permit
item.description: There appear to be new clues about the location of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding.

item.title: Hundreds of veterans snag free World Cup tickets
item.description: A program for veterans, current military and first responders secured almost 5,000 free tickets for World Cup matches.

item.title: Sioux Falls mayoral candidates separated by 2 votes: "Every vote matters"
item.description: Christine Erickson is two votes ahead of Jamie Smith, the Minnehaha County Auditors and Elections Office said.

item.title: Apple and Microsoft are raising their prices as chip costs soar
item.description: Apple is raising the prices of some MacBooks and iPads, while Microsoft is raising Xbox prices as semiconductor costs surge.

item.title: WNBA suspends Alyssa Thomas for hitting Caitlin Clark's throat
item.description: No foul was initially called after Alyssa Thomas' fist made contact with Caitlin Clark's throat during a game between the Phoenix Mercury and Indiana Fever on Wednesday.

item.title: Iran strikes vessel in Strait of Hormuz amid debate over "transit fees"
item.description: The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps struck a cargo ship near Oman on Thursday, further complicating the Iran-U.S. negotiations.

item.title: Powerful earthquakes strike Venezuela, hundreds dead or injured
item.description: Venezuela's acting president said the death toll from powerful twin earthquakes was likely to rise, as USGS modeling suggested thousands may have been killed.

item.title: Big Tech is all in on AI. Now all they need is customers.
item.description: Technology companies are betting trillions of dollars that consumers will open their wallets for AI services. But what if Big Tech is wrong?

item.title: Anderson Cooper's farewell to 60 Minutes
item.description: In a heartfelt sign-off, Cooper reflects on the stories, risks, adventures, and human connections that defined his two decades with television's most iconic newsmagazine.

item.title: Anderson Cooper's emotional farewell to 60 Minutes after 20 years
item.description: In a heartfelt sign-off, Cooper reflects on the stories, risks, adventures, and human connections that defined his two decades with television's most iconic newsmagazine.

item.title: How recycling could help power America's rare earth future
item.description: One untapped resource to meet the rising need for rare earth elements: recycling what's already been used.

item.title: How the Savannah Bananas celebrate foster families
item.description: The exhibition baseball league Banana Ball focuses on entertaining people of all ages – including "Bananas Foster," a nonprofit that honors foster families.
